question: Anthropic 最近的企业市场进展
answer:
近期，Anthropic在企业级市场取得了突破性进展。凭借密集的产品发布、战略收购以及对下沉市场的精准拓展，Anthropic不仅在付费客户份额上首次超越了最大竞争对手OpenAI，还构建了极具竞争力的企业服务生态。以下是其近期在企业市场的核心进展分析：

**1. 企业付费客户份额首超OpenAI，估值逼近万亿美元**
根据Ramp最新发布的AI指数数据，Anthropic在企业市场的渗透率迎来了爆发式增长。目前有34.4%的受访企业正在付费使用Anthropic的服务，首次超越了OpenAI的32.3%。在过去12个月内，Anthropic的企业付费占比从9%大幅攀升至26%，而同期OpenAI的份额微降了1%。自2025年5月以来，Anthropic的企业级市场份额已实现四倍增长。
在强劲的商业表现支撑下，Anthropic目前正推进一轮规模达数百亿美元的融资，预计将使其估值达到约9500亿美元（作为对比，OpenAI在2026年3月融资时的估值为8540亿美元）。
（参考来源：[1] ）

**2. 战略下沉：推出Claude小微企业版（Claude for Small Business）**
在巩固大型企业客户的同时，Anthropic正将AI平台竞争的重心向下沉市场转移，瞄准了占美国GDP 44%、总数达3600万家的小微企业。
2026年5月中旬，Anthropic依托其Claude Cowork平台正式推出了“Claude小微企业版”。该套件深度集成了Intuit QuickBooks、PayPal、HubSpot、Canva、Docusign、Google Workspace和Microsoft 365等主流商业软件。
*   **核心功能**：内置15项开箱即用的智能体工作流（Agentic Workflows）和15项针对高频重复任务的专属技能，覆盖财务对账、薪酬规划、营销投放、合同审查等核心业务环节。
*   **安全与合规**：系统严格采用“人工发起+前置审批”机制（即在发送、发布或付款前需人工确认），继承原有平台权限，且默认不将企业数据用于模型训练。
*   **市场推广**：为加速获客，Anthropic启动了全美10城巡回推广（首站设在芝加哥），每站为100名当地小微企业主提供免费的AI素养培训与工作坊。
（参考来源：[2] ）

**3. 产品形态演进：从被动问答转向“主动式AI”与智能体管理**
Anthropic产品负责人Cat Wu明确表示，公司未来的战略是保持在技术最前沿，而非被动跟随竞品。未来半年，产品重心将从同步交互全面转向自动化例程与智能体管理。
*   **主动式AI**：未来的AI将在用户意识到需求之前主动做出响应。未来的工作模式将演变为“人类员工管理智能体舰队（fleets of agents）”，这要求企业管理者必须具备深厚的领域专业知识，以有效调试和管理智能体指令。
*   **顶尖企业专属的安全模型**：针对头部企业对极致安全的诉求，Anthropic在2026年4月启动了“Glasswing”计划。该计划仅向Amazon、Apple、CrowdStrike和Microsoft等少数核心合作伙伴开放了名为“Mythos”的全新网络安全模型。该模型专门用于扫描代码库中的软件漏洞，因能力过于强大，Anthropic出于防范恶意利用的考量未将其向公众发布。
（参考来源：[3] ）

**4. 收购Stainless，夯实企业级开发者工具链生态**
在开发者生态建设方面，Anthropic于2026年5月18日正式宣布收购成立于2022年的SDK与MCP（模型上下文协议）服务器工具供应商Stainless。
Stainless此前长期负责为Anthropic API生成涵盖TypeScript、Python、Go等多语言的官方开发工具包与命令行接口。此次并购旨在将Stainless的SDK生成能力与Anthropic的模型连接框架进行深度整合，从而大幅扩展Claude智能体对企业内部数据与外部工具的调用能力，进一步强化企业级平台的开发体验。
（参考来源：[4] ）

**总结**
Anthropic近期的企业市场动作展现了高度的战略清晰度：向上通过Mythos等前沿模型绑定科技巨头，向下通过Claude小微企业版和全美巡回培训快速收割中小企业市场，底层则通过收购Stainless巩固开发者生态。这一系列组合拳使其在商业化进程上首次实现了对OpenAI的局部反超，并为未来的智能体自动化办公时代奠定了基础。

Title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/13/anthropics-cat-wu-says-that-in-the-future-ai-will-anticipate-your-needs-before-you-know-what-they-are/

Published Time: 2026-05-13T19:28:06+00:00

Markdown Content:
With the tech industry singularly focused on AI models, Anthropic is having an exceptionally good year.

The company may soon pull ahead of its main competitor, as it looks to raise tens of billions of dollars in a funding round that would put its valuation at some $950 billion (OpenAI was valued at $854 billion in its March round), and business customers increasingly express a prefererence for Claude over ChatGPT. A recent report showed Anthropic recently outpaced OpenAI among business customers, quadrupling its market share since May 2025.

Cat Wu, Anthropic’s head of product for Claude Code and Cowork, has been a key figure in that success. Since joining the company in August 2024, Wu has helped shepherd Claude through a critical phase, leveling it up from a purely informational chatbot to a coding tool and beyond. Wu, who oversees the development of new features, is frequently paired with Boris Cherny, a core member of Anthropic’s technical staff and the creator of Claude Code, leading the pair to be characterized as Anthropic’s “Batman and Robin.”

Wu sat down with me at last’s week’s second annual Code with Claude conference in San Francisco, where she discussed how she thinks about product strategy, and how she hopes the experience of using Claude will change in the future.

This interview has been edited for length and clarity.

**When you’re looking at product strategy, how much of it is reactive to your peers or your competitors? Do you think about that at all?**

The main thing that we design for is staying on the exponential, so I think, across our team, we instill in everyone the lesson that AI will just continue to get better. For us, we just need to stay at this frontier. We don’t think about competitors. I think if you do think about competitors, you end up being, like, perpetually two weeks, or like, a month behind how fast you can execute. And so it’s normally not the best way to stay at the frontier.

**Anthropic released at least six models last year and has already released almost as many this year. Do you expect this pace of development to continue?**

Our hope is that it continues (laughing). I think the models are still improving at a very steady pace, and so we should be able to keep sharing those with our users. I think the deployments might look a bit different—like how we handled Glasswing, but as much as possible, we want this intelligence to benefit as many people as possible, and it has to be handled in a very safe way, which is why we handled Glasswing [in the way that we did].

[_Glasswing is an initiative that Anthropic launched in April that invited a small consortium of partner organizations — including companies like Amazon, Apple, CrowdStrike, and Microsoft— to gain access to its new cybersecurity model, Mythos. Unlike many of Anthropic's other AI models, Mythos is not being given a general public release. The company has claimed that it fears the model — which is designed to scan codebases for software vulnerabilities — is too powerful, and could be weaponized by bad actors._]

**You said in a previous interview that the future of work is basically staff managing fleets of agents. It seems like that could eventually lead to a situation where the agents are better at the job, or know the job, better than the human.**

I think it is extremely hard to manage agents if you can't do the job yourself. I think the managers still need to be experts in their domain. It's a new skill set that a lot of people are going to have to learn, but managing agents is actually very similar to being a manager of people, in the sense that you have to understand, like, why did th

Title: Introducing Claude for Small Business

URL Source: https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-for-small-business

Markdown Content:
We're launching Claude for Small Business—a package of connectors and ready-to-run workflows that put Claude inside the tools small businesses depend on—to help small business owners take full advantage of AI and cross off items on the to-do list.

Small businesses account for 44% of U.S. GDP and employ nearly half the private-sector workforce, but their adoption of AI has lagged behind larger enterprises. Tools and training are rarely tailored to the ways small businesses operate, and as a result their use often stops at the chat window. As part of our public benefit mission, we are committed to helping business owners harness AI more fully and effectively for their most important work.

Claude for Small Business is a toggle install that puts Claude to work inside the tools small business owners already use: Intuit Quickbooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Canva, Docusign, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365. From these tools, it can plan payroll, close the month, run a sales campaign, chase invoices, and more.

_“Small businesses make up nearly half the American economy, but they've never had the resources of bigger companies. AI is the first technology that can finally close that gap, which is why we're launching Claude for Small Business, alongside training and partnerships to make sure AI shows up for the entrepreneurs and communities who need it most. Claude for Small Business runs inside the tools owners already rely on, like QuickBooks, PayPal, and HubSpot, and takes on the work that piles up after hours, like planning payroll, chasing invoices, or kicking off a marketing project. People run the business, and Claude helps take the late-night work off their plates.”_

_—Daniela Amodei, Co-founder and President of Anthropic_

## **How it works**

Toggle on Claude for Small Business inside Claude Cowork, connect the tools you already use, and pick the job. Claude does the work; you approve before anything sends, posts, or pays.

It ships with **15 ready-to-run agentic workflows** across finance, operations, sales, marketing, HR, and customer service. It also includes **15 skills built on the repeatable tasks** owners told us slow them down most.

These include:

*   **Planning payroll with confidence.** Settle your QuickBooks cash position against incoming PayPal settlements, build a 30-day forecast, rank what's overdue, and queue the reminders for you to approve and send.
*   **Closing the month with fewer errors.** Reconcile your books against settlements, flag what doesn't match, write a plain-English P&L, and export a close packet you can forward straight to your accountant through Intuit QuickBooks.
*   **Getting a pulse on your business.** Surface your most important business insights on a schedule, all on one page: view your cash position through Intuit QuickBooks, sales trend, pipeline movement, this week's commitments, and more.
*   **Running your next campaign.** Find the slow stretch in your revenue, analyze your HubSpot campaign performance, draft the promo strategy, and generate the assets in Canva to prepare your next send.

Title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/13/anthropic-courts-a-new-kind-of-customer-small-business-owners/

Published Time: 2026-05-13T16:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
Anthropic is looking to court smaller companies. To that end, the company announced Wednesday the launch of Claude for Small Business, a new suite of services designed for customers who less resemble Walmart and Starbucks and more resemble the local hardware store or coffee shop.

So far, much of the most intensive AI adoption has occurred at the enterprise level. In the recent past, studies have shown that most companies that scaled AI systems beyond experimental or pilot-level integration tended to be large companies with expansive budgets. This appears to be changing somewhat, as smaller and mid-sized businesses are seeing greater adoption.

Anthropic’s new bundle of features are designed to serve those new AI converts. They are available via a newly introduced toggle within Claude Cowork, the company’s task-automation platform for business users that can browse the web, manage files, and execute multi-step workflows on a user’s behalf. By toggling it on, paying users gain access to a host of automated services, including bookkeeping functions, business insights, and generative tools for ad campaigns.

The new suite also includes integrations between Claude Cowork and a number of software products — like QuickBooks, Canva, DocuSign, HubSpot, and PayPal.

“Small businesses account for 44% of U.S. GDP and employ nearly half the private-sector workforce, but their adoption of AI has lagged behind larger enterprises,” the company said. “Tools and training are rarely tailored to the ways small businesses operate, and as a result their use often stops at the chat window.”

For founders and investors, the move signals that the AI platform wars are expanding downmarket and that the next major battleground for user acquisition isn’t the Fortune 500; it’s the 36 million small businesses that make up the backbone of the U.S. economy.

Anthropic is a little behind its competitor, OpenAI, which launched Enterprise ChatGPT at the end of 2023, including an integration for smaller teams called ChatGPT Business.

Anthropic is planning to aggressively promote its new features with a coast-to-coast promotional tour, starting in Chicago and hitting 10 cities in total. At each stop, the company plans to offer a free AI training workshop that will be available to 100 local small business leaders.

_When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn’t affect our editorial independence._

Lucas is a senior writer at TechCrunch, where he covers artificial intelligence, consumer tech, and startups. He previously covered AI and cybersecurity at Gizmodo. You can contact Lucas by emailing lucas.ropek@techcrunch.com.

question: NVIDIA 最近在 AI 芯片上有什么动作
answer:
根据2026年5月的最新行业动态，NVIDIA 最近在 AI 芯片及其相关生态领域动作频频。其战略已经从单纯的 GPU 硬件迭代，全面升级为“底层架构创新、集群运维管理、垂直场景加速与巨额资本布局”四位一体的综合护城河。以下是 NVIDIA 最近的核心动作细节：

### 1. 硬件架构突破：推出 Vera Rubin 平台并联手 Groq 解决扩展瓶颈
针对多智能体（Agentic AI）和万亿参数混合专家（MoE）模型在推理时带来的高并发与长上下文扩展难题，NVIDIA 推出了新一代 **Vera Rubin NVL72** 平台，并史无前例地与 **Groq 3 LPX** 进行了芯片与编译器的深度协同设计。
*   **核心参数**：该平台采用了 LPU C2C 高速直连架构，实现了单机架高达 **640 TB/s** 的互联带宽与 **128 GB** 的统一 SRAM 池。
*   **性能飞跃**：在 400K 长上下文场景下，该架构打破了吞吐量与延迟的权衡瓶颈。其吞吐量比上一代 **GB200 NVL72 提升了 35 倍**，能够为高端 AI 服务提供确定性的超低延迟。
*(来源：[1] )*

### 2. 算力集群运维：发布 Fleet Intelligence 托管服务
随着数据中心 GPU 集群规模的指数级增长，NVIDIA 于2026年5月11日正式推出了 **Fleet Intelligence** 托管服务，专为企业提供实时的 GPU/CPU 集群监控与优化能力。
*   **技术特性**：该服务基于低占用的开源 Agent，整合了 GPUd、DCGM（数据中心 GPU 管理器）及 Attestation SDK 技术。
*   **全面兼容**：原生支持 **Blackwell、Hopper 以及最新的 Vera Rubin** 芯片架构。
*   **核心功能**：聚焦功耗、温度、性能、健康状态（如 ECC 和 XID 错误）与固件安全验证，帮助企业在多租户、高负载环境下避免热节流，最大化算力资源的投资回报率（每瓦性能）。

### 3. 垂直领域落地：GB200 芯片加速前沿科学计算与车载 AI
NVIDIA 的最新 AI 芯片正在加速向极端数据处理和边缘计算场景渗透：
*   **科学计算（XANI 工作流）**：NVIDIA 团队利用 **32 台 GB200 Grace Blackwell** 超算芯片集群，大幅优化了 XANI（X射线纳米成像）数据分析工作流。通过 cuPyNumeric 分布式框架与 GPUDirect 存储技术，将 **42TB** 多维实验数据的处理时间从传统的 **9个月大幅压缩至 4小时内**，实现了 **1000倍** 的计算提速与 **165倍** 的 I/O 吞吐提升。
*   **车载智能体**：基于 **DRIVE AGX Thor** 多域计算方案，NVIDIA 推出了从云端到车端的 AI 智能体架构。结合 TensorRT-LLM 与 TensorRT Edge-LLM 工具链，实现了低于 **500毫秒** 的响应延迟，为车企提供低延迟、高隐私的生产级多模态智能体方案。

### 4. 商业版图扩张：超 400 亿美元的 AI 股权投资
除了技术研发，NVIDIA 在资本市场的动作同样极具侵略性，通过资金反哺上下游生态。据2026年5月9日的数据显示，NVIDIA 在今年初已向 AI 企业承诺了**超过 400 亿美元**的股权投资，参与了约 **24 轮**私营初创公司的融资。
*   **重磅投资明细**：其中包括向 **OpenAI 注资 300 亿美元**，向玻璃制造商 **Corning 投资 32 亿美元**，以及向数据中心运营商 **IREN 投资 21 亿美元**。分析师指出，这种深度的资本绑定有助于 NVIDIA 锁定未来的算力需求，构建难以逾越的竞争优势。

## 来源

Title: How the NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform is Solving Agentic AI’s Scale-Up Problem

URL Source: https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/how-the-nvidia-vera-rubin-platform-is-solving-agentic-ais-scale-up-problem/

Published Time: 2026-05-14T19:24:35+00:00

Markdown Content:
Agentic inference has fundamentally changed the runtime dynamics of inference workloads by introducing non-deterministic trajectories—actions, observations, and decisions that an AI agent produces while working through a task. These trajectories compound end-to-end latency across hundreds of inference requests per session.

NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 handles the bulk of that inference load as the core compute engine of the NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform. The most demanding emerging multi-agent workloads require sustained low-latency and high-throughput generation on trillion-parameter MoE models with long-context windows.

Until now, no platform has served this emerging workload economically. NVIDIA Groq 3 LPX, paired with Vera Rubin NVL72, is the first to deliver both high throughput and low latency at this point on the Pareto curve.

This post explores how the NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform solves this challenge through extreme co-design, combining high-throughput compute with low-latency, deterministic execution across hundreds to thousands of chips.

## Why agentic workloads require predictable scale-up networking

Conventional data center networking fabrics are optimized for large training jobs and volume inference workloads, where small amounts of network jitter average out inside large batches. Premium AI services, by contrast, demand higher model capability and highly responsive user-visible performance. At this tier, agentic decode brings a fundamentally different set of requirements, including:

*   Multi-turn model requests
*   Smaller batches
*   Extremely low latency

Long context and large MoE models (used in premium AI services) introduce additional networking challenges (Figure 1). Each agent in a multi-agent pipeline carries its own expanding KV cache, system prompt, tool definitions, and conversation history. That KV cache and any new tokens must be routed through trillion-parameter models and their associated experts across different accelerators.

_Figure 1. Conceptual diagram showing how agentic workloads grow context and state. Frontier MoE models require parallelism. Both depend on fast, deterministic inter-processor networking_

To pull this off, ‌network-level orchestration must ensure minimal variability in the hops between chips. This cross-chip exchange is unavoidable in any SRAM-based architecture that can’t hold the model on a single chip. The physical mechanism by which the exchange occurs becomes a key bottleneck in the serving system.

The industry has traditionally addressed this challenge by using:

*   **Runtime-arbitrated networking fabrics**where flow control is reactive, and timing is statistically bounded rather than guaranteed.
*   **Large concentrations of on-die compute and memory** that postpone the networking problem until model and context window sizes require them to scale up and out, resulting in deteriorating multi-chip performance.

Breaking the throughput-latency tradeoff at agentic scale requires networking fabric designed with the silicon, compiler, and serving stack. The LPU C2C achieves this with extreme co-design enabling multi-trillion parameter models at scale.

## How NVIDIA Groq 3 LPX addresses scale-up challenges

The NVIDIA Groq 3 LPX LPU C2C is designed for solving scale-up problems directly. Rather than treating interconnects as a conventional network that must absorb contention and timing uncertainty at runtime, LPU C2C extends Groq’s deterministic execution model across many LPUs. It does this through three tightly connected technologies:

*   High-radix point-to-point links
*   LPU Compiler-scheduled data movement
*   Hardware-driven plesiosynchronous timing

Together, these technologies enable

Title: Introducing NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence for Real-Time GPU Fleet Visibility and Optimization

URL Source: https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/introducing-nvidia-fleet-intelligence-for-real-time-gpu-fleet-visibility-and-optimization/

Published Time: 2026-05-11T19:44:27+00:00

Markdown Content:
The compute capability of large GPU fleets presents unprecedented opportunities to innovate and provide value to customers in record time. Yet these advancements come with a variety of challenges. At scale, teams are juggling heterogeneous hardware, fast‑moving software stacks, tight power envelopes, and spiky, multitenant workloads. A single hotspot, misconfigured driver, or subtle hardware fault can ripple, causing throttled jobs, missed SLAs and wasted spend.

As well, the complexity and number of components involved in large-scale clusters can be daunting, so it’s essential to maintain visibility into the day-to-day operations and understand the operational state at any given time. Monitoring GPU utilization and identifying bottlenecks during job execution becomes more difficult. Identifying areas of low utilization and migrating workloads to them is one of the best ways to ensure the highest return on investment.

For these reasons, GPU‑aware monitoring is essential at scale. Teams need visibility beyond whether or not the node is up. They need to know whether, at any given moment, every accelerator is performing as expected, safely, and consistently.

This post introduces NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence, an agent-based managed service for continuous monitoring of NVIDIA data center GPUs. It is now generally available.

## What are the key focus areas of GPU monitoring?

Important areas of GPU monitoring include power, temperature, performance, health, and uniform configuration.

*   **Power**: Track power utilization and throttling to stay within data center budgets while maximizing performance per watt.
*   **Temperature**: Detect hotspots and airflow issues early to avoid thermal throttling and premature component aging.
*   **Performance**: Watch utilization, memory bandwidth, interconnect health, and throttling reasons to spot regressions and imbalance across the fleet.
*   **Health**: Surface ECC and XID errors, retired pages, HBM/NVLink/PCIe anomalies, and other RAS signals to catch failing parts before they fail.
*   **Uniform configuration and integrity**: As part of GPU inventory validation, check for consistent drivers, firmware, and BIOS settings to ensure reproducible results and safe operation, as well as verify firmware integrity.

## What is NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence?

NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence is a low-level, deployment-agnostic managed service that can be used regardless of software stack or scheduler choice. Initially, the service supports data center GPU and CPU customers that are managing their own infrastructure, and engineers requiring more insight into GPU and CPU behavior.

The service leverages technology and IP from across the NVIDIA portfolio of products and learnings from running the NVIDIA fleet of hundreds of thousands of GPUs across NVIDIA DGX Cloud.

Fleet Intelligence uses a low-footprint, host-based agent to stream GPU telemetry back to the fully managed Fleet Intelligence cloud service. NVIDIA is releasing the Fleet Intelligence agent as an open source project for auditability. The agent leverages other NVIDIA open source solutions such as GPUd, NVIDIA Data Center GPU Manager (DCGM), and the NVIDIA Attestation SDK. To learn more, visit NVIDIA/fleet-intelligence-agent on GitHub. Fleet Intelligence has been developed with feedback from early access (EA) customers, including NVIDIA Cloud Partners (NPCs), Lambda and IREN.

This GA release focuses on three main areas:

*   Inventory and visualization
*   Reporting, alerts, and health checks
*   Integrity and attestation

### Inventory and visualization

Fleet Intelligence offers a rich capability to visualize global fleet inventory across dat

Title: Accelerated X-Ray Analysis for Nanoscale Imaging (XANI) of Novel Materials

A massive-scale X-ray free-electron laser (XFEL) enables tracking structural and electron dynamics in novel systems, including fusion materials, semiconductors, batteries, and catalysis. It produces ultrashort X-ray pulses that can record the movements of atoms and electrons. These instruments can detect the smallest change in material structure caused by defects and other influences. The high repetition rate of these bright X-ray bursts can reach up to 1 million shots per second with 35-million-pixel cameras.

The acquired multidimensional datasets contain rich physical information about the fastest microscopic movements of electrons and atoms, which can help identify defects in materials. Processing and analyzing these datasets to extract the physics has conventionally required more than nine months of computational time.

XFEL research facilities include SwissFEL in Switzerland, Spring-8 Angstrom Compact free-electron Laser (SACLA) in Japan, Linac Coherent Light Source (LCLS-II) at SLAC, European XFEL in Germany, and Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PAL) in Korea.

This post highlights new technical breakthroughs of the Accelerated X-ray Analysis for Nanoscale Imaging (XANI) workflow. The NVIDIA team demonstrated on characterization of quantum materials to reconstruct the phonon dispersion from ultrafast femtosecond laser pump/hard X-ray probe experiments.

Specifically, the team accelerated the XANI workflow and compressed the computational time to process and analyze 42 terabytes (TBs) of data shrinks from nine months to less than four hours on 32 NVIDIA GB200 Grace Blackwell Superchips, while preserving the same precision of acquired data. The XANI project has been adopted by different communities, from quantum physics to materials chemistry, demonstrating the ability of CUDA Python and distributed computing to accelerate scientific discoveries.

_Figure 1. Real-time movies of the ultrafast small movements with 3-kilometer linear accelerator_

## What are the challenges of single-node Python for exascale science?

Massive-scale XFEL facilities can operate up to megahertz (MHz) rates and generate hundreds of TBs to petabytes (PBs) of data. This massive volume of data must be processed and analyzed in real time to steer scientific experiments and accelerate discovery.

Traditional CPU-bound pipelines require significant manual parameter tuning and subsampling, often only processing 10% of a dataset during an experiment. For high-resolution imaging of new phases in quantum materials, the computational cost of nonlinear fitting and 3D reconstruction previously relegated analysis to the post-experiment phase. A single experiment could require nine months of computational time.

## How does XANI accelerate numerical computation and I/O performance?

From the originally vectorized NumPy and SciPy, the NVIDIA team accelerated the XANI workflow 43x on a single GPU on a GB200 Grace Blackwell Superchip and 1,000x on 64 GPUs. As a result, the computational time to process and analyze 42 TBs of data shrinks to less than four hours, while preserving the same precision of acquired data.

To achieve this improvement, new cuPyNumeric libraries were developed, including LMFIT and multithreaded Hierarchical Data Format 5 (HDF5). These libraries further improve GPU utilization for numerical computation and 165x acceleration in I/O throughput with GPUDirect Storage (GDS) and multithreaded HDF5.

_Figure 2. XANI code performance shows strong scaling results on x86 CPUs compared to GPUs on GB200 Grace Blackwell Superchips_

question: Claude 最近有哪些更新
answer:
近期关于 Claude 的更新主要集中在模型服务稳定性、Claude Code 的企业级最佳实践、垂直领域（如法律）的开源生态扩展，以及社区对订阅机制的反馈。以下是截至 2026 年 5 月中旬的具体动态汇总：

**1. 模型与服务稳定性修复**
2026年5月15日，Anthropic 官方通报并解决了一起服务端常规运维异常。该异常曾导致 Claude Opus 4.6、Opus 4.7 以及 Sonnet 4.6 的 API 请求错误率显著升高，并直接影响了 Claude API (api.anthropic.com) 和 Claude Code 服务的正常运行。经过技术团队的排查与修复，目前各模型的请求成功率已逐步恢复正常，该事件已标记为解决。
（来源：[1]）

**2. Claude Code 在大型代码库中的最佳实践**
2026年5月14日，Anthropic 发布了关于 Claude Code 在大型企业代码库中部署与运行的官方指南。文章指出，在处理数百万行代码的单体仓库或分布式架构时，Claude Code 采用了“代理搜索（Agentic Search）”来替代传统的 RAG（检索增强生成），从而有效避免了索引延迟。其核心架构依赖五大扩展层：
*   **CLAUDE.md**：提供分层的项目上下文。
*   **钩子（Hooks）**：实现工作流的自动化改进。
*   **技能（Skills）**：按需加载特定领域的专业知识。
*   **插件（Plugins）**：用于分发标准化的团队配置。
*   **MCP 与 LSP 集成**：通过模型上下文协议（MCP）和语言服务器协议（LSP）扩展外部工具与符号级导航。
官方建议企业通过目录级初始化，并设立专属的 AI 工具管理角色，以最大化上下文效率与团队采用率。
（来源：[2]）

**3. 生态扩展：开源法律工作流与开发者认知插件**
*   **Claude for Legal 开源套件**：2026年5月14日，Anthropic 在 GitHub 正式开源了 `claude-for-legal` 插件套件。该项目专为 Claude Code 与 Managed Agents 设计，涵盖商业、企业、隐私、诉讼及 AI 治理等 10 余个垂直领域，内置超过 70 个命名智能体（Agents）与定时工作流。系统支持通过 MCP 协议无缝对接 CourtListener、Westlaw 及 Ironclad 等主流法律数据平台。所有生成内容均内置引用溯源与合规审查机制，代码遵循 Apache-2.0 协议，采用纯 Markdown 与 JSON 配置，无需编译即可部署。
*   **开发者刻意练习插件**：同日，社区推出了一款针对 Claude Code 与 Codex 的开源技能插件。该工具基于认知科学设计，当开发者完成文件创建或架构重构等关键节点后，会自动触发 10 至 15 分钟的互动练习（应用预测、生成、检索实践与间隔重复等技术）。其核心目的是抵消过度依赖 AI 生成代码所带来的“流畅性错觉”及元认知缺失等学习风险。

**4. 社区争议：订阅机制与历史数据访问权限**
在技术社区方面，2026年5月13日 HackerNews 上引发了一场关于 Claude 订阅机制的广泛讨论。有用户反馈，在取消 Claude Code Max 订阅后，立即失去了对过往“Claude Design”历史项目的访问权限，且此前因服务不稳定而获得的补偿积分也无法保留。技术分析指出，这主要源于复杂的计费合同与动态用量限制逻辑产生的边界缺陷。这一事件引发了开发者对 SaaS 工具“厂商锁定（Vendor Lock-in）”风险的担忧，社区普遍建议用户在使用此类 AI 编程工具时，务必提前备份核心代码与项目数据。
（来源：[3]）

## 来源
- [1] [[AI] Claude Opus 与 Sonnet 模型请求错误率异常已解决](https://status.claude.com/incidents/8z7l5zcy0v3b)
- [2] [[AI] Claude Code在大型代码库中的工作原理及最佳实践](https://claude.com/blog/how-claude-code-works-in-large-codebases-best-practices-and-where-to-start)
- [3] [[AI] HN用户反映取消Claude订阅后丢失历史项目访问权限](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48128003)

Title: Tell HN: Dont use Claude Design, lost access to my projects after unsubscribing

URL Source: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48128003

Markdown Content:
I wanted to try codex after 5 months of claude code max subscription. And then I went back to my previous projects on claude design only to realize I don't have access to them anymore.

This is a first. I never lost access to any of my past sessions because I unsubscribed in any of the LLM apps.

I actually wanted to try out codex previously, but had similar experience with my credits. They gave extra credits equivalent to my montly subscription price, with some time limit because claude has so many issues that month. And as soon as plan ended. I lost access to the credits. Even after resubscribing, I still don't have access to those credits.

I have sympathies towards the engineers, especially the ones that are putting themselves on X. But only when someone with large following has some issue, they sort it out.

Having worked at a billing company, I can see how complex contracts sound good for the growth/sales folks but are also horrible for engineers actually implementing those contracts. Their complex rate limiting which is now a norm, identifying other harnesses to count them against extra usage are all probably not easy to implement without very rough edge cases. But all the "bugs" are just where the user gets screwed is what is problematic.

I just wanted to post this here, after tagging them multiple times on X to alert other users.

question: Google Gemini 最近的进展
answer:
Google Gemini 在最近（2026年4月至5月期间）取得了多项重大进展，其应用范围已从底层代码生成、初创生态扶持，全面扩展至车载系统与个人健康管理等消费级场景。以下是 Gemini 近期核心进展的详细梳理：

### 1. 车载系统全面升级：Gemini 进驻数百万辆汽车
2026年4月30日，谷歌宣布将 Gemini AI 助手引入搭载 Google 内置系统的汽车，正式取代原有的 Google Assistant。
* **覆盖规模**：通用汽车（GM）已确认将该功能推送至约 400 万辆 2022 年及更新款的车型（涵盖凯迪拉克、雪佛兰、别克和 GMC 等品牌）。该功能不仅限于新车，兼容的旧款车型也可通过 OTA 软件升级获取。
* **核心功能**：Gemini 提供了更自然的免提对话体验。用户可以结合 Google Maps 提出复杂需求（例如“寻找沿途评价高、有户外座位且提供素食的餐厅”），并直接通过语音控制车内温度、获取车辆手册信息，或对收到的长信息进行摘要和语境化回复。
* **Gemini Live**：支持实时、开放式连续对话的 Gemini Live 也已在车端开启 Beta 测试。该车载系统首发面向美国英语用户，后续将扩展多语言并深度集成 Gmail、Google 日历和智能家居生态。
*(信息来源：[1] )*

### 2. 个人健康管理：Google Health Coach 全球上线
基于 Gemini 大模型构建的 Google Health Coach 于2026年5月19日正式面向全球上线，标志着 Gemini 在垂直医疗健康领域的深度落地。
* **服务内容**：提供高度个性化的健身、睡眠、营养指导以及医疗记录摘要。系统支持语音、图像及 PDF 等多模态数据录入，并整合了经期追踪与心理健康模块。
* **技术与隐私**：引入了 SHARP 评估框架以严格保障健康建议的准确性，谷歌同时承诺用户的健康数据绝不会被用于广告推送。
* **定价与硬件**：该服务集成于 Google Health Premium 计划，定价为 9.99 美元/月或 99 美元/年（Google AI Pro 及 Ultra 用户可免费使用），初期无缝兼容 Fitbit 与 Pixel Watch 硬件设备。

### 3. 前沿科研与商业落地：代码智能体 AlphaEvolve 效能爆发
2026年5月7日，Google DeepMind 披露了由 Gemini 驱动的代码智能体 AlphaEvolve 的最新数据，展现了其在跨领域复杂问题上的强大推理与优化能力：
* **科研与基建突破**：AlphaEvolve 将基因测序变异检测的错误率大幅降低了 30%，使电网优化模型的可行解比例从 14% 跃升至 88% 以上，并将自然灾害预测准确率提高了 5%。在谷歌内部基础设施方面，它优化了下一代 TPU 硅片设计，使 Google Spanner 的写入放大率降低 20%，软件存储占用减少近 9%。
* **商业化提速**：在企业端，AlphaEvolve 帮助金融科技公司 Klarna 的模型训练速度翻倍，使 Substrate 的光刻仿真提速数倍，并实现了 10.4% 的物流路由效率提升以及 4 倍的材料科学推理加速。

### 4. 构建 AI 商业生态：第二届 Gemini 初创企业论坛
为了进一步扩大 Gemini 的行业影响力，谷歌于2026年5月13日宣布了第二届 Gemini 初创企业论坛（Gemini Startup Forum）的入选名单。
* **入选阵容**：从全球近 2000 份申请中，谷歌筛选出了来自美国、英国、法国、印度、新加坡等 16 个国家的 102 家 AI 初创企业。这些企业主要聚焦于制造智能、临床工作流及可穿戴硬件等前沿落地场景。
* **资源扶持**：入选团队受邀前往谷歌桑尼维尔总部参加峰会。谷歌为这些企业提供了最高 35 万美元的 Google Cloud 云服务积分、API 开发冲刺指导、Google AI Studio 专属权限以及技术专家的直接产品策略优化，全面加速 Gemini 衍生项目的研发与规模化部署。
*(信息来源：[2] )*

## 来源
- [1] [[AI] 谷歌将Gemini AI助手引入数百万辆汽车](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/30/googles-gemini-ai-assistant-is-hitting-the-road-in-millions-of-vehicles/)
- [2] [[AI] Google宣布第二届Gemini初创企业论坛入选名单](https://blog.google/company-news/outreach-and-initiatives/entrepreneurs/2026-gemini-startups-forum/)

Title: Meet the 100+ startups joining our second Google for Startups Gemini Startup Forum

URL Source: https://blog.google/company-news/outreach-and-initiatives/entrepreneurs/2026-gemini-startups-forum/

Representing 16 countries, these 102 startups are at the forefront of the AI revolution.

D

Darren Mowry

VP, Global Startups, Google Cloud

## General summary

Google is hosting 102 startups from 16 countries at its Sunnyvale headquarters for a two-day summit focused on scaling artificial intelligence solutions. These founders will collaborate with experts to refine their products and tackle technical challenges. You can learn more about this cohort and the available cloud credits, training, and resources on the Gemini Startup Forum page.

Summaries were generated by Google AI. Generative AI is experimental.

## Bullet points

*   Check out "Meet the 100+ startups joining our second Google for Startups Gemini Startup Forum."
*   Over 100 global startups are heading to Google for a two-day growth summit.
*   These founders will use AI to solve complex problems in manufacturing and healthcare.
*   Selected from 2,000 applicants, these companies represent 16 countries leading the AI revolution.
*   Google supports these startups with $350,000 in cloud credits and expert technical training.

Summaries were generated by Google AI. Generative AI is experimental.

## Basic explainer

Google is bringing over 100 startups to its headquarters to help them grow their businesses. These companies are using AI to solve big problems in areas like healthcare and technology. Google will also give these teams money and tools to help them build their products faster. It’s a great way for new creators to get the support they need to succeed.

More than 100 startups will convene at Google’s Sunnyvale headquarters next month for a two-day in-person summit designed to accelerate their growth.

At the inaugural Google for Startups Gemini Startup Forum — launched in partnership with Google DeepMind last November — more than 50 pioneering founders collaborated with experts across Google to overcome technical challenges, refine their product strategies and scale their solutions globally. Now, the second cohort of startups has been selected from nearly 2,000 applications from around the globe.

Representing 16 countries — including major tech hubs such as the United States, the United Kingdom, France, India, Singapore and Brazil — these 102 startups are at the forefront of the AI revolution. While they tackle diverse challenges, from transforming manufacturing intelligence and streamlining clinical workflows to building the next generation of wearable hardware, they share a common mission: leveraging AI to create more adaptive, intelligent and scalable solutions for the modern world.

At the Gemini Startup Forum, participants will hear from Google AI experts and leaders, receive hands-on support from the engineers behind Google’s newest products, gain exclusive access to AI demos and network with fellow founders.

Learn more about the 2026 cohort on the Gemini Startup Forum page.

The Google for Startups Gemini Startup Forum is part of our global Gemini Kit suite of resources. We’re also accelerating the development of eligible AI startups by providing them with up to $350,000 in Cloud credits, which they can use across a broad range of Google Cloud services, along with hands-on API sprints, a multimedia library with trainings and learning materials and access to Google AI Studio.

Title: Google’s Gemini AI assistant is hitting the road in millions of vehicles

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/30/googles-gemini-ai-assistant-is-hitting-the-road-in-millions-of-vehicles/

Published Time: 2026-04-30T17:46:54+00:00

Markdown Content:
Google announced on Thursday that it will begin rolling out Gemini to cars with Google built-in, marking a significant upgrade from the current Google Assistant. The move signals Google’s push to bring more advanced, conversational AI into the driving experience.

The announcement follows closely behind news from General Motors, which revealed yesterday that Gemini is coming to approximately 4 million vehicles from model year 2022 and newer, spanning brands like Cadillac, Chevrolet, Buick, and GMC. However, today’s announcement didn’t name specific automakers, suggesting that Gemini won’t be limited to GM vehicles.

The rollout will begin in the U.S. with English-language support, with availability expanding over the coming months. Notably, Gemini isn’t just reserved for new vehicles, but also for compatible existing cars via software updates.

Cars with Google built-in first launched in 2020. Now, according to Google, the new Gemini update enables a more natural, conversational way for drivers to interact with their vehicles.

Soon, drivers will be able to speak more freely to complete tasks, explore ideas, or retrieve information. For instance, a driver could say they want to stop for lunch at a highly rated sit-down restaurant with outdoor seating along their route. Gemini can pull relevant suggestions using data from Google Maps, then handle follow-up questions like parking availability or menu options, including dietary preferences.

Gemini can also complete tasks like turning on the heat, provide directions, recommend music, retrieve vehicle information, as well as summarize incoming messages, and help drivers respond hands-free.

Additionally, there’s Gemini Live, which is currently in beta and allows for more open-ended, real-time conversations. Drivers can activate it by tapping a button in the interface or by saying, “Hey Google, let’s talk,” enabling brainstorming, learning, or general discussion while on the road.

Techcrunch event

San Francisco, CA|October 13-15, 2026

Drivers who are signed into their Google accounts in compatible vehicles will be prompted with the option to upgrade. Once enabled, Gemini can be accessed through voice commands, the on-screen microphone, or steering wheel controls.

Google says it plans to expand Gemini support to additional languages and regions. Future updates are also expected to deepen integration with services like Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Home.

## General summary

Gemini is replacing Google Assistant in cars with Google built-in, offering a more natural, conversational way to manage navigation, music, and vehicle settings. This update is rolling out to both new and existing vehicles, starting with English-language users in the United States. Keep an eye on your dashboard for an upgrade prompt once you are signed into your Google account.

## Basic explainer

Google is adding Gemini to cars with built-in Google software to replace the old voice assistant. You can now talk to your car naturally to find restaurants, control settings or even learn facts about your trip. It works for both new and existing cars through a simple software update. It’s designed to make driving easier and more helpful while you’re on the road.

Summaries were generated by Google AI. Generative AI is experimental.

Gemini — your helpful AI assistant — is starting to roll out in cars with Google built-in as an upgrade from Google Assistant. With Gemini, you can speak naturally to get more done with your favorite apps, brainstorm ideas and even ask for vehicle-specific info thanks to Gemini's integration with your car.

When cars with Google built-in first hit the road in 2020, we made a commitment that your car will get better over time. That means that Gemini is coming not only to new cars, but also to existing ones through a software update. The rollout will start with English in the United States and continue over the coming months.

## Speak naturally to get more done

Say goodbye to rigid voice commands. You can have a natural conversation with Gemini, hands-free.

*   **Find the perfect pit stop:** Whether you’re craving a specific food or a scenic spot, just tell Gemini what you’re looking for. For example: "I need to grab lunch, find some highly rated sit-down restaurants along the way. I'm not in a rush, oh, and I’d like to eat outside." Gemini will reference information from Google Maps to find options, then you can follow up with questions like, "What’s the parking like?_”_ or “Do they have vegetarian options?”
*   **Know what’s ahead:** Relevant, real-time updates about your journey from Google Maps empower you to make informed decisions and drive with confidence. Ask, “I'm driving by the stadium. Is there an event happening and should I avoid it because of traffic?” And if you see a traffic disruption, help others by reporting, “I see an accident in the right lane.”
*   **Stay on top of your messages**: Ask Gemini to summarize your new text messages so you can respond with context and confidence. Tell Gemini: "Reply to Jane that I'm on my way and add my ETA._"_ And if you change your mind, you can edit your message without starting over: "Actually, also ask her if I should pick up dessert."

question: Meta AI 最近的动态
answer:
近期，Meta 在其人工智能（Meta AI）的布局和产品集成方面动作频频，主要集中在旗下社交平台（Threads、WhatsApp、Instagram 和 Facebook）的功能拓展、隐私保护以及合规性审查上。以下是 Meta AI 最近的核心动态与具体细节：

### 1. Threads 测试集成 Meta AI，但“不可屏蔽”引发争议
2026年5月12日，Meta 宣布在 Threads 平台上测试类似于 X 平台 Grok 的 Meta AI 集成功能。
* **功能机制**：拥有公开账户的用户可以在帖子或回复中直接提及 `@meta.ai`，以获取关于实时趋势、突发新闻的背景信息或建议（例如询问体育赛事赛况或热门话题）。Meta AI 会以公开回复的形式，使用与原帖相同的语言进行解答。
* **测试范围**：该功能目前正在马来西亚、沙特阿拉伯、墨西哥、阿根廷和新加坡这五个国家进行 Beta 测试。
* **用户争议**：功能上线后，大量用户发现官方并未提供屏蔽（Block）该 AI 账号的入口，尝试屏蔽时会直接触发系统错误。Meta 官方表示，如果用户不想看到 AI 的回复，目前只能通过“静音（Mute）”、“不感兴趣”或直接隐藏单条回复来处理。这一强制性限制引发了广泛的用户投诉与负面反馈。
*(来源：[1] )*

### 2. WhatsApp 推出 Meta AI“隐身模式”与新模型 Muse Spark
为了满足用户在咨询财务、健康等私密问题时的隐私需求，Meta 于2026年5月13日宣布在 WhatsApp 及独立的 Meta AI 应用中推出“隐身对话（Incognito Mode）”功能。
* **隐私机制**：用户在与 Meta AI 的一对一聊天中点击新图标即可开启隐身会话。该模式在隔离的安全环境中处理数据，默认不保存任何聊天记录。一旦用户关闭会话、关闭应用或锁屏，消息将自动消失，AI 也会彻底清除该特定对话的上下文记忆。
* **底层技术**：该功能调用了 Meta 在2026年4月最新发布的 **Muse Spark** 模型，而非此前使用的小型模型。
* **后续规划**：WhatsApp 产品副总裁 Alice Newton-Rex 透露，Meta 正在开发名为“Side Chat”的新功能，未来将允许用户在多人聊天群组中隐蔽地调用 Meta AI 提问，而不会惊动群内的其他成员。
*(来源：[2] )*

### 3. 部署 AI 识别未成年用户（身高与骨骼结构分析）
在儿童安全合规压力下（此前 Meta 因儿童安全问题被美国新墨西哥州处以 3.75 亿美元的民事罚款），Meta 于2026年5月5日宣布部署一套全新的 AI 系统，用于识别 Facebook 和 Instagram 上未满 13 岁的违规用户。
* **技术手段**：该 AI 系统明确排除了人脸识别技术，而是通过分析用户上传的图像与视频中的“身高”和“骨骼结构”等视觉特征，并结合文本和互动上下文来推断用户年龄。
* **执行措施**：该系统目前已在部分国家试点，被 AI 疑似为未成年的账户将被直接停用，直到用户通过官方的年龄验证。同时，Meta 正在将包含默认隐私设置与消息限制的“严格青少年账户模式”扩展至欧盟 27 国与巴西，并计划于6月登陆英美地区。

### 4. 前高管入局 AI 评估领域
作为行业背景补充，前 Meta 新闻主管 Campbell Brown 于近期创立了初创公司 Forum AI，并获得了 300 万美元的融资。该公司专门训练“AI 评委”来评估大语言模型在地缘政治、金融等高风险议题上的准确性与偏见，目前其 AI 评委与人类专家共识率已达到 90% 的目标指标。这反映出 Meta 及其前核心团队对当前大模型信息源偏差和政治偏见问题的持续关注。

总体而言，Meta 正试图通过最新的 Muse Spark 模型将 AI 深度嵌入其庞大的社交生态中，一方面通过 Threads 提升平台活跃度与信息检索能力，另一方面通过 WhatsApp 的隐身模式和未成年人识别 AI 来对冲日益严峻的隐私与合规风险。

Title: Threads tests a Meta AI integration that works similarly to Grok

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/12/threads-tests-a-meta-ai-integration-that-works-similarly-to-grok/

Published Time: 2026-05-12T16:34:36+00:00

Markdown Content:
Threads is testing a Meta AI integration that works similarly to X’s Grok. Users with a public account will be able to mention Meta AI in a post or a reply to get more context. The feature is currenty in beta testing in Malaysia, Saudi Arabia, Mexico, Argentina, and Singapore.

Meta told TechCrunch in an email that the feature is designed to help people get real-time context about trends and breaking stories, as well as receive recommendations, all within conversations.

Now, users can mention Meta AI to ask questions like, “why are people talking about the World Cup this month?, “whose Met Gala looks are trending right now?” or “how are the Knicks doing in the playoffs?” Meta AI will then process the invocation and respond as a public reply authored by the @meta.ai account. Meta AI will respond in the language used in the post it was mentioned in.

By integrating Meta AI into its platform, Threads is positioning itself as not just a destination for chatting about news and trends, but also a place where you can get information and recommendations without having to leave the app.

The idea is similar to Grok’s role on X, which is filled with posts of users asking the AI chatbot questions like “is this real?” or “explain this.” Of course, giving an AI chatbot this level of visibility carries risks, as seen on X when Grok generated posts praising Hitler. Still, Meta AI notably has stronger safeguards in place than Grok, though it remains to be seen whether it will be prone to similar issues.

Meta notes that if you want to see fewer Meta AI replies in your feed, you can mute @meta.ai, use the “Not interested” option on any Meta AI post, or hide a Meta AI reply that appears directly on your post.

The company says it plans to learn from early feedback and will continue improving the experience before expanding it to more people.

_When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn’t affect our editorial independence._

Aisha is a consumer news reporter at TechCrunch. Prior to joining the publication in 2021, she was a telecom reporter at MobileSyrup. Aisha holds an honours bachelor’s degree from University of Toronto and a master’s degree in journalism from Western University.

You can contact or verify outreach from Aisha by emailing aisha@techcrunch.com or via encrypted message at aisha_malik.01 on Signal.

Title: WhatsApp adds an incognito mode in Meta AI chats

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/13/whatsapp-adds-an-incognito-mode-in-meta-ai-chats/

Published Time: 2026-05-13T14:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
Meta on Wednesday said it is adding the ability to start “incognito” conversations with its Meta AI chatbot within WhatsApp. These conversations, the company said, will be processed in a secure environment and can’t be seen by anyone.

Users can start an incognito session by tapping on a new icon in one-on-one chats with Meta AI. The company said the feature will also be available on the standalone Meta AI app as well.

Incognito chats will roll out to WhatsApp and the Meta AI app over the next few months.

Meta said these incognito conversations are not saved, and messages will disappear by default once you close the chat. The session will also end if you close the app or lock your phone, and Meta AI will lose the context of that particular conversation, the company said.

“People are starting to use AI for everything, including some of their most private thoughts, whether that’s tackling financial or health questions, or for advice on how to respond to a tricky message from a friend or a colleague. We think it’s really important to give people the ability to ask these questions as privately as possible,” Alice Newton-Rex, VP of Product at WhatsApp, told TechCrunch over a call.

The company has been laying down the groundwork for secure AI chats on WhatsApp for a while now. Last year, it detailed its private processing infrastructure that would let it build AI features without breaking end-to-end encryption. Since then, WhatsApp has added features like AI-powered summaries of messages that use this architecture.

Newton-Rex said Meta used smaller models to power its previous features, but the new incognito chat uses its latest Muse Spark model, which was released last month.

The company is already working on its next feature that taps its private processing infra. Called Side Chat, it will let users invoke Meta AI within chats to ask questions and get answers privately without notifying or showing it to other people in the chat.

Currently, you need to tag a message and ask a question to the AI assistant to get an answer that other participants in the chat can see. If you privately need to ask a question, you have to paste the text in a separate chat window.

ChatGPT and Claude offer incognito modes, too, and companies like DuckDuckGo and Proton have launched their own privacy-first chatbots.

Meta's move towards private AI chats comes at a key time. Last month, Reuters cited lawyers who opined that users' conversations with an AI chatbot could be used against them in litigation.

# Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads

Users can tag Meta AI to get answers to questions, but a lot of people don’t want to see it.

Users can tag Meta AI to get answers to questions, but a lot of people don’t want to see it.

is a senior reporter covering technology, gaming, and more. He joined The Verge in 2019 after nearly two years at Techmeme.

Meta announced on Tuesday that it’s testing a Threads feature that lets users tag a Meta AI account to get answers to questions or context about a conversation on the platform. If you’ve spent any time looking at replies on X as of late, this new feature sounds a lot like Meta’s take on people tagging xAI’s Grok. But, as reported by _Engadget_, Threads users quickly discovered that you can’t block the new Meta AI account, and they aren’t happy about it.

Meta has invested heavily in AI as it works to catch up to rivals like OpenAI and Google, spending billions to hire AI talent. It launched a new AI model called Muse Spark in April, which it said it would bring to its apps and services, and now, it’s showing up on Threads with the new Meta AI account. question: DeepSeek 最近发布了什么
answer:
DeepSeek 最近发布了备受瞩目的 **V4 系列预览版模型**，主要包括 **DeepSeek-V4-Pro** 和 **DeepSeek-V4-Flash** 两款。此次发布在开源社区和 AI 行业引起了巨大反响，其核心亮点在于“接近前沿的性能”与“极具破坏性的低价”。

以下是本次发布的具体细节：

**1. 模型参数与架构**
两款模型均采用混合专家（MoE）架构，支持高达 100 万 Token 的上下文窗口，且均采用标准的 MIT 协议开源权重：
*   **DeepSeek-V4-Pro**：总参数量高达 1.6 万亿（1.6T），激活参数为 490 亿（49B）。它超越了 Kimi K2.6（1.1T）和 GLM-5.1（754B），成为目前最大的开源权重模型。其在 Hugging Face 上的权重大小为 865GB。
*   **DeepSeek-V4-Flash**：总参数量为 2840 亿（284B），激活参数为 130 亿（13B），权重大小为 160GB。

**2. 极具竞争力的定价**
V4 系列的 API 定价大幅低于同级别的行业前沿模型：
*   **Flash 模型**：输入为 0.14 美元/百万 Token，输出为 0.28 美元/百万 Token。这一价格甚至低于 OpenAI 的 GPT-5.4 Nano（0.20美元/1.25美元）和 Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite。
*   **Pro 模型**：输入为 1.74 美元/百万 Token，输出为 3.48 美元/百万 Token。作为大型前沿模型，其价格远低于 GPT-5.4（2.50美元/15美元）和 Claude Sonnet 4.6（3美元/15美元）。

**3. 性能与底层优化**
DeepSeek 在 V4 版本中极大地优化了长上下文的推理效率。在 100 万 Token 上下文场景下：
*   **Pro 模型**的单 Token FLOPs 仅为上一代 V3.2 的 27%，KV 缓存占用仅为 10%。
*   **Flash 模型**的单 Token FLOPs 更是降至 V3.2 的 10%，KV 缓存占用仅为 7%。
在基准测试中，V4 模型的推理能力已接近行业顶尖水平，官方预计其性能仅落后于最新前沿模型（如 GPT-5.4 和 Gemini-3.1-Pro）约 3 到 6 个月（详细评测见：[1] ）。

**4. 繁荣的开发者生态**
伴随 V4 的发布，开源社区迅速跟进推出了相关工具：
*   **DS4 本地推理引擎**：知名开发者 antirez 开源了专为 Apple Metal 架构定制的 ds4 引擎。通过 2-bit 非对称量化技术，仅需 128GB 内存（如 M3 Max）即可在本地流畅运行 V4 Flash 模型，短文本生成速度可达 26.68 tokens/s，长上下文为 21.47 tokens/s。
*   **DeepClaude**：一款开源工具，可通过临时注入环境变量，将 Claude Code CLI 的后端无缝切换至 DeepSeek V4 Pro。结合上下文缓存技术，可使重度开发者的 API 成本降低 75% 至 90%。

**5. 商业动态：估值暴涨**
在技术突破的同时，DeepSeek 正在进行首轮外部融资。据报道，其估值在短短数周内从 200 亿美元跃升至 450 亿美元。本轮融资由国家集成电路产业投资基金牵头，腾讯与阿里巴巴亦在洽谈参与。此举旨在通过向员工授股应对人才流失，并依托华为芯片生态加速国产 AI 技术自主化（来源：[2] ）。

## 来源

Title: DeepSeek could hit $45B valuation from its first investment round

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/06/deepseek-could-hit-45b-valuation-from-its-first-investment-round/

Published Time: 2026-05-06T17:20:34+00:00

Markdown Content:
In Brief

Posted:

10:20 AM PDT · May 6, 2026

**Image Credits:**CN-STR/AFP / Getty Images

DeepSeek is in talks to raise its first round of venture capital, and in just a few weeks, its potential valuation has soared from $20 billion to $45 billion, the Financial Times and Bloomberg reported.

The Chinese AI lab came to prominence in early 2025 after launching a large language model that trained on a fraction of the compute power and at a fraction of the cost of the big U.S. models like those from OpenAI and Anthropic. It has since kept reasonable pace with the top models in the world in areas like reasoning and coding while remaining open weight (versions are freely available on Hugging Face).

Founded by Chinese hedge fund billionaire Liang Wenfeng, who controls nearly 90% of the company, the lab has not previously sought out investors, the FT reports. However, faced with competitors poaching DeepSeek’s researchers, Liang opted to raise funds in order to offer employees shares in the company, sources tell the FT.

The round is said to be led by the state investment vehicle China Integrated Circuit Industry Investment Fund, Bloomberg reports. China is seeking to fund homegrown AI technology to sidestep the difficulty of obtaining U.S. technology, particularly chips. DeepSeek has been optimized to run on chips made by China’s hardware giant Huawei Technologies. That combo is considered a powerful duo for the nation to develop its own AI to rival the United States. The country’s cloud giants Tencent and Alibaba are also reportedly in talks to participate, per Bloomberg.

DeepSeek could not be immediately reached for comment.

Markdown Content:
# DeepSeek V4—almost on the frontier, a fraction of the price

# Simon Willison’s Weblog

Subscribe

**Sponsored by:**MongoDB — Join MongoDB.local London 2026 on 7 May to learn how teams move AI from prototype to production. 

## DeepSeek V4—almost on the frontier, a fraction of the price

24th April 2026

Chinese AI lab DeepSeek’s last model release was V3.2 (and V3.2 Speciale) last December. They just dropped the first of their hotly anticipated V4 series in the shape of two preview models, DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash.

Both models are 1 million token context Mixture of Experts. Pro is 1.6T total parameters, 49B active. Flash is 284B total, 13B active. They’re using the standard MIT license.

I think this makes DeepSeek-V4-Pro the new largest open weights model. It’s larger than Kimi K2.6 (1.1T) and GLM-5.1 (754B) and more than twice the size of DeepSeek V3.2 (685B).

Pro is 865GB on Hugging Face, Flash is 160GB. I’m hoping that a lightly quantized Flash will run on my 128GB M5 MacBook Pro. It’s _possible_ the Pro model may run on it if I can stream just the necessary active experts from disk.

For the moment I tried the models out via OpenRouter, using llm-openrouter:

```
llm install llm-openrouter
llm openrouter refresh
llm -m openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro 'Generate an SVG of a pelican riding a bicycle'
```

Here’s the pelican for DeepSeek-V4-Flash:

And for DeepSeek-V4-Pro:

For comparison, take a look at the pelicans I got from DeepSeek V3.2 in December, V3.1 in August, and V3-0324 in March 2025.

So the pelicans are pretty good, but what’s really notable here is the _cost_. DeepSeek V4 is a very, very inexpensive model.

This is DeepSeek’s pricing page. They’re charging $0.14/million tokens input and $0.28/million tokens output for Flash, and $1.74/million input and $3.48/million output for Pro.

Here’s a comparison table with the frontier models from Gemini, OpenAI and Anthropic:

| Model | Input ($/M) | Output ($/M) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **DeepSeek V4 Flash** | $0.14 | $0.28 |
| GPT-5.4 Nano | $0.20 | $1.25 |
| Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite | $0.25 | $1.50 |
| Gemini 3 Flash Preview | $0.50 | $3 |
| GPT-5.4 Mini | $0.75 | $4.50 |
| Claude Haiku 4.5 | $1 | $5 |
| **DeepSeek V4 Pro** | $1.74 | $3.48 |
| Gemini 3.1 Pro | $2 | $12 |
| GPT-5.4 | $2.50 | $15 |
| Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3 | $15 |
| Claude Opus 4.7 | $5 | $25 |
| GPT-5.5 | $5 | $30 |

DeepSeek-V4-Flash is the cheapest of the small models, beating even OpenAI’s GPT-5.4 Nano. DeepSeek-V4-Pro is the cheapest of the larger frontier models.

This note from the DeepSeek paper helps explain why they can price these models so low—they’ve focused a great deal on efficiency with this release, especially for longer context prompts:

> In the scenario of 1M-token context, even DeepSeek-V4-Pro, which has a larger number of activated parameters, attains only 27% of the single-token FLOPs (measured in equivalent FP8 FLOPs) and 10% of the KV cache size relative to DeepSeek-V3.2. Furthermore, DeepSeek-V4-Flash, with its smaller number of activated parameters, pushes efficiency even further: in the 1M-token context setting, it achieves only 10% of the single-token FLOPs and 7% of the KV cache size compared with DeepSeek-V3.2.

DeepSeek’s self-reported benchmarks in their paper show their Pro model competitive with those other frontier models, albeit with this note:

> Through the expansion of reasoning tokens, DeepSeek-V4-Pro-Max demonstrates superior performance relative to GPT-5.2 and Gemini-3.0-Pro on standard reasoning benchmarks. Nevertheless, its performance falls marginally short of GPT-5.4 and Gemini-3.1-Pro, suggesting a developmental trajectory that trails state-of-the-art frontier models by approximately 3 to 6 months.

I’m keeping an eye on huggingfa

question: xAI 最近有什么新动作
answer:
近期，马斯克旗下的 xAI 在商业模式、资本运作、技术迭代以及合规方面均有密集且重大的新动作。综合最新情报，xAI 正处于一个关键的转型期，具体表现在以下几个核心维度：

**1. 业务重心向“新云（Neocloud）”模式转型，与 Anthropic 达成重大算力交易**
2026年5月上旬，xAI 宣布与竞争对手 Anthropic 达成了一项令人意外的战略合作。Anthropic 全面接管并买下了 xAI 位于田纳西州孟菲斯的 Colossus 1 数据中心的全部算力容量（规模约 300MW）。
这一交易标志着 xAI 的商业模式发生了重大转变：从单纯的消费者 AI 模型开发商，转变为算力基础设施提供商（即新云模式）。由于 Grok 模型在消费端和企业端的需求疲软，xAI 选择将庞大的算力资产直接变现，这笔交易预计价值数十亿美元。马斯克对此解释称，xAI 自身的模型训练已经转移到了更新的 Colossus 2 数据中心，因此不再需要同时保留两个数据中心的算力。

**2. 筹备 IPO 与 SpaceX 的深度整合**
xAI 在2026年1月的融资中估值已高达2300亿美元。目前，xAI 正在与 SpaceX 进行合并，业内甚至预计 xAI 可能会结束其作为独立机构的存在，被完全整合进 SpaceX。与 Anthropic 的算力租赁交易被市场广泛视为 SpaceX 筹备 IPO 前的“估值预热”和资产负债表优化举措。此外，xAI 还在推进更宏大的基础设施计划，包括在 Terafab 制造自研芯片以降低对英伟达的依赖，以及计划到2035年部署太空数据中心。

**3. 密西西比数据中心因违规排放遭环保起诉**
在基础设施快速扩张的同时，xAI 也面临严峻的合规挑战。2026年5月13日，美国全国有色人种协进会（NAACP）联合南方环保法律中心对 xAI 提起诉讼，并向法院申请了禁令。起因是 xAI 在其密西西比州的数据中心运行了多达46台天然气燃气轮机，其中仅有15台获得了官方许可。xAI 试图利用法律漏洞，将这些发电设备放置在平板拖车上，从而让州政府将其归类为“移动”设备，以此规避为期一年的空气污染监管。原告指控此举违反了联邦法规，并严重加剧了该地区的空气污染。

**4. 发布 Grok 4.3 模型，并承认使用 OpenAI 技术进行“模型蒸馏”**
在技术与产品端，xAI 于2026年5月1日正式发布了 Grok 4.3 模型的 API 文档与定价。该模型性能大幅提升，支持高达 100万 tokens 的上下文窗口，内置函数调用、结构化输出及深度推理能力。其定价策略极具侵略性：输入为 1.25美元/百万 tokens（缓存输入仅 0.20美元），输出为 2.50美元/百万 tokens，系统速率限制为每分钟 1800 次请求与 1000万 tokens。

值得注意的是，在2026年4月30日加州联邦法院的一场庭审中，马斯克出庭作证时明确承认，xAI 在训练 Grok 模型时，采用了针对 OpenAI 模型的“蒸馏技术”（Model Distillation）。这种在行业内引发争议的做法帮助仅有数百人团队规模的 xAI 显著降低了算力成本，并快速缩短了与头部企业的技术差距。

# Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center

Elon Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 natural gas turbines at its Mississippi data center, power plants that the state is currently not regulating thanks to a loophole.

The power plants are considered “mobile” by the state of Mississippi because they are sitting on flatbed trailers, thus allowing them to dodge to air pollution regulations for one year. The NAACP, which has filed a lawsuit on behalf of residents in the area, says the unchecked emissions from the turbines is worsening air quality in an already polluted region. This week, it asked the court for an injunction against xAI.

At issue is the “mobile” nature of the turbines. The Southern Environmental Law Center, which filed the lawsuit on behalf of the NAACP, says the turbines are being operated in violation of federal law, which says that power plants mounted on a trailer can still be considered stationary and subject to air pollution regulations.

XAI has been granted permits for 15 of its turbines. A Greater Memphis Chamber of Commerce press release previously said that “about half” of the 35 turbines in operation in May 2025 would remain on site. However, xAI has continued to install more. Currently, it’s operating 46, according to a local news report.

Topics

AI, air pollution, Climate, data centers, Elon Musk, natural gas, xAI

Title: Is xAI a neocloud now?

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/06/is-xai-a-neocloud-now/

Published Time: 2026-05-06T21:32:55+00:00

Markdown Content:
On Wednesday, xAI and Anthropic announced a surprise partnership that has the Claude-maker buying out “all of the compute capacity at [xAI’s] Colossus 1 data center,” roughly 300MW that allowed Anthropic to immediately raise its usage limits. It’s a huge deal for xAI, likely worth billions of dollars. More importantly, it immediately monetized one of the company’s most impressive accomplishments, turning xAI from a consumer to a provider of compute.

It’s tempting to see the arrangement as a shot at OpenAI amid the ongoing lawsuit. But Musk’s explanation on X was that xAI had already moved training to a newer data center, Colossus 2, and xAI simply didn’t need the both.

In the short term, there’s an obvious logic at work. xAI’s existing products are mostly focused on Grok, which has seen plummeting usage since the image generation debacles earlier this year. If xAI’s data center buildout is that much more than what Grok needs to operate, partnering with Anthropic adds a lot of green to the balance sheet. This is especially useful as the company, now combined with SpaceX, speeds towards an IPO. More broadly, having Anthropic lined up as a customer makes it easier to believe that SpaceX’s orbital data center play might actually work.

But beyond the short-term benefit, the Anthropic partnership sends an unusual message about where Elon Musk’s priorities really lie. It suggests the company’s real business may be more about building data centers than training AI models.

It’s rare to see a major tech company treat compute resources this way when companies like Google, Meta who are also training models, are building more data centers. It’s an easy point to miss, because so many of these companies are working as enterprise AI vendors, online services and cloud providers all at once. But when forced to make a choice between selling more available compute to customers and preserving some to build their own tools, they reliably choose door #2.

Just last month, Sundar Pichai admitted on a call that Google Cloud revenue was lower than it could have been because the company was “capacity constrained” — and when given the choice of renting out their GPUs or using them to develop AI products, Google chose the AI products.

Facebook has faced a more extreme version of the same constraint, spinning up an entirely new cloud apparatus just to ensure they would have enough GPU power to chase Zuckerberg’s AI ambition. As he put it when announcing Meta Compute in January, “How we engineer, invest, and partner to build this infrastructure will become a strategic advantage.”

Techcrunch event

San Francisco, CA|October 13-15, 2026

The key word there is “strategic.” Both Zuckerberg and Pichai are looking towards a future where AI is powering the most popular and lucrative systems in the world. Computing power isn’t just a way to satisfy today’s inference demand, but to build tomorrow’s products — and running short on compute means missing out on that chance.

By focusing on data centers (earthbound and otherwise), xAI is positioning itself more like a neocloud business: buying GPUs from Nvidia and renting them out to model developers like Anthropic. It’s a far more difficult business, squeezed by both chip suppliers and the shifting cycles of demand. The valuations for most active neoclouds reflect that reality: xAI was valued at $230 billion in its January funding round; Coreweave, which oversees a comparable quantity of computing power, is worth less than a third of that.

Musk’s version of a neocloud is more ambitious, as you might expect. Some of the data centers might be in space — at least by 2035, if things go according to plan. xAI will be making its own chips at the Terafab, which will take away some but not all of Nvidia’s pricing power. Title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/10/were-feeling-cynical-about-xais-big-deal-with-anthropic/

Published Time: 2026-05-10T15:34:25+00:00

Markdown Content:
Anthropic and xAI announced a big partnership this week, with Anthropic buying all the compute capacity at xAI’s Colossus 1 data center in Tennessee.

On the latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Kirsten Korosec, Sean O’Kane, and I discussed what the deal might mean for xAI’s parent company SpaceX, as SpaceX prepares to go public and apparently plans to dissolve xAI as a separate organization.

Kirsten did her best to offer “a positive view” on the partnership — after all, it’s a new way for xAI to make money. But she also noted that this also suggests xAI isn’t doing much when it comes to training its own frontier AI models, and it’s harder for the company to position itself as a “forward-looking, innovative” business when that’s the case.

Then Sean asked: “Why be positive when you can be cynical?” In his view, this seems like “a major heat check before the IPO.” Yes, becoming a neocloud might be “a more believable business in the near term,” but it’s less likely to get outside investors excited in the long term. (And then there’s the environmental lawsuit that xAI is facing over Colossus 1.)

Keep reading for a preview of our conversation, edited for length and clarity.

**Sean O’Kane:** I always love a surprise, especially when everybody’s eyes [are] on another ball, a major trial that’s happening. Seemingly out of nowhere this week, SpaceX and therefore its AI subsidiary xAI — which apparently no longer exists now, or is imminently not about to exist, which we can get to — struck a deal with Anthropic.

Basically, the real version of the deal is that Anthropic’s essentially taking over all of the compute at the data center known as Colossus 1 in Memphis, Tennessee, to focus on Anthropic’s more enterprise-focused AI products. There’s been a lot of reporting about how [Anthropic’s] been looking for more compute […] and it seems like an escape valve for them to be able to strike this deal and get access to all this compute.

Techcrunch event

San Francisco, CA|October 13-15, 2026

In the near term, for xAI and for SpaceX, yes, they are a neocloud now, in the sense that they had to do something with all this compute that they were building, because it certainly seems like they were not going to need it for Grok — which, outside of X, is not burning up the world as far as becoming the new hot consumer chat bot.

**Kirsten Korosec:** And we should say that in terms of what a neocloud is, for those who don’t know, this is the idea of buying GPUs from Nvidia and the like, and renting those out as opposed to using those for their own AI, training their own AI models.

So this is a different kind of business, and the point that our AI editor, Russell Brandom, makes is that a lot of companies are building out data centers, but if given a choice between, do they rent them out [or using them to train their own models], they are still prioritizing using this compute for their own internal AI model training. I think that’s an important point and one that suggests that maybe xAI isn’t doing so much on the AI model training [side]

**Anthony Ha:** Right, and as Sean was alluding to, most people would not necessarily think of Grok as — not only that it’s known for some pretty unpleasant, if not downright illegal, content, but also it’s not necessarily super cutting edge. Especially if we start talking about enterprise AI, which I know we’re gonna be getting into later in this episode, you don’t hear a lot about people using Grok for work-critical tasks.

And so the question becomes: How can xAI actually make money? And apparently just selling the infrastructure could be one of the main ways to do it.

question: 对比 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 最近的企业策略
answer:
OpenAI 和 Anthropic 作为当前生成式 AI 领域的两大头部企业，其近期的企业策略展现出了截然不同的侧重点。根据最新的市场动态与数据（2026年5月），OpenAI 正致力于多模态 API 的扩展，但深陷公司治理与法律纠纷的泥潭；而 Anthropic 则在积极通过收购完善开发者生态、下沉中小企业市场，并严格管控其股权交易。

以下是两家公司近期企业策略的详细对比：

**一、 产品与市场扩张策略**

1. **OpenAI：深化多模态能力与 API 赋能**
OpenAI 近期的产品策略继续围绕其核心大模型的多模态能力展开。2026年5月7日，OpenAI 在其 API 中推出了新型语音智能功能（Voice Intelligence Features）。这表明 OpenAI 正在加速将语音等复杂模态集成到开发者生态中，以维持其在底层模型能力上的技术壁垒和商业化领先地位。

2. **Anthropic：完善工具链与发力中小企业（SMB）市场**
相比之下，Anthropic 的产品策略更加注重“落地”与“开发者体验”。
*   **完善开发者生态**：2026年5月18日，Anthropic 宣布收购 AI 开发工具链公司 Stainless。这一收购策略显示出 Anthropic 正在从单纯的模型提供商向全栈 AI 开发平台转型，意图降低开发者使用其模型的门槛。
*   **拓展下沉市场**：2026年5月14日，Anthropic 推出了面向中小企业的 Claude 智能体工作流套件（Claude for small business）。这表明 Anthropic 正在避开与 OpenAI 在大型企业客户上的正面红海竞争，转而通过提供开箱即用的智能体工作流，抢占具有庞大基数的中小企业市场。

**二、 公司治理与资本运作策略**

1. **OpenAI：面临治理结构与信任危机的严峻考验**
OpenAI 近期的企业精力在很大程度上被高管层的法律纠纷所牵扯。马斯克诉 OpenAI 及 Sam Altman 一案已于2026年5月14日进入陪审团裁决阶段。庭审的核心焦点直指 Sam Altman 的个人可信度以及 OpenAI 复杂的公司治理结构。这种高调的法律诉讼不仅消耗了公司的管理资源，也让外界对其非营利与营利混合架构的稳定性产生了持续质疑。

2. **Anthropic：严格的资本管控与投资者关系维护**
在资本层面，Anthropic 展现出了对公司股权和估值的强力控制欲。2026年5月12日，Anthropic 官方发出警告，要求投资者切勿通过未授权的二级市场平台交易其股份（详见：[1] ）。这一举措旨在防止公司估值在非正规市场中被恶意炒作或低估，确保其在未来潜在融资或 IPO 时的资本主动权。

**三、 舆论数据与市场情绪对比**

从近期的市场监测数据来看，两家公司的舆论表现也印证了上述策略带来的影响：
*   **曝光量与关注度**：OpenAI 的近期相关报道量高达 185 篇，远超 Anthropic 的 39 篇。这说明 OpenAI 依然是行业的绝对流量中心。
*   **市场情绪（Sentiment）**：OpenAI 的负面情绪占比较高（在 185 篇中，正面 68 篇，中性 85 篇，负面 32 篇），这主要归因于马斯克的诉讼案带来的负面公关效应。而 Anthropic 的舆论环境则健康得多（39 篇中，正面 17 篇，中性 19 篇，负面仅 3 篇），市场对其收购 Stainless 和推出中小企业套件等务实举措给予了高度认可（相关 HackerNews 讨论热度分别达到了 452 和 488 分）。

**总结**

总体而言，OpenAI 目前的策略是“技术突破与防御并重”，在继续推进前沿多模态技术（如语音 API）的同时，不得不花费大量精力应对历史遗留的治理结构危机。而 Anthropic 则处于“稳健扩张期”，通过收购工具链公司和推出中小企业智能体工作流，稳扎稳打地构建商业护城河，并在资本市场上保持高度的纪律性。两者的竞争正在从单纯的模型参数比拼，全面转向生态建设、市场细分与企业治理的综合较量。

question: 对比 NVIDIA 和 AMD 最近的市场表现
answer:
作为资深科技情报分析师，基于过去14天的核心市场监测数据与行业动态，为您深度对比 NVIDIA 与 AMD 最近的市场表现。

整体而言，NVIDIA 在市场讨论热度、资本布局规模以及软硬件生态护城河的建设上继续保持绝对领先；而 AMD 则表现出相对平稳但略显被动的态势，近期主要面临底层架构安全漏洞带来的公关与技术逆风。

以下是具体的量化数据与战略动态对比分析：

### 一、 市场关注度与情绪量化指标对比

从近期的开发者社区、科技媒体（如 HackerNews, TechCrunch, AWS ML Learning 等）的数据采样来看，两家公司的市场声量呈现显著差异：

*   **讨论基数与热度**：NVIDIA 在监测期内获得了 174 次核心提及，平均热度得分为 151.2 分；相比之下，AMD 获得了 106 次提及，平均热度得分为 143.6 分。NVIDIA 的绝对声量比 AMD 高出约 64%。
*   **市场情绪分布**：
    *   NVIDIA 的情绪分布为：正面 54 条，中立 80 条，负面 40 条。
    *   AMD 的情绪分布为：正面 32 条，中立 60 条，负面 14 条。
    *   两家公司均以中立的技术讨论为主，但 NVIDIA 获得了更多的正面评价，这主要归功于其密集的生态合作与投资动作。
*   **动能变化（Momentum）**：NVIDIA 的近期讨论动能下降了 31.1%（指标从 103 降至 71），而 AMD 的动能仅下降了 7.3%（从 55 降至 51）。这表明 NVIDIA 在经历前期极高的市场狂热后，正处于常态化的热度回调阶段；而 AMD 的基本盘关注度则相对固化和稳定。

### 二、 NVIDIA：巨额资本开路与软件生态深化

NVIDIA 近期的市场表现不仅局限于硬件算力的垄断，更体现在其作为“AI 生态造王者”的战略扩张上：

1.  **激进的资本布局**：根据 2026年5月9日 的市场披露，NVIDIA 在今年初至今已在 AI 领域投入了超过 400 亿美元的股权投资。这种规模的资本运作表明 NVIDIA 正在通过直接投资绑定未来的核心 AI 独角兽，确保其硬件在下一代 AI 基础设施中的绝对统治地位。
    *   原始来源参考：[1]
2.  **软件与集群管理能力的护城河**：2026年5月11日，NVIDIA 推出了 Fleet Intelligence 服务，专门用于实现 GPU 集群的实时监控与优化。这直击大型云厂商和 AI 实验室在万卡集群管理上的痛点。
3.  **模型训练管线优化**：2026年5月7日，知名开源项目 Unsloth 宣布与 NVIDIA 达成合作，共同优化大语言模型（LLM）的训练管线，综合提速达到约 25%（来源：[2]）。这种软硬件协同优化进一步压缩了竞争对手的生存空间。

### 三、 AMD：底层安全隐患与生态突围的挑战

相比 NVIDIA 的高歌猛进，AMD 近期的市场表现受到了一些负面安全事件的拖累，同时在底层编译生态上持续寻找突破口：

1.  **核心架构面临安全信任危机**：2026年5月18日，安全研究人员公开披露了一个严重漏洞，攻击者可利用 Infinity Fabric 的错误配置直接攻破 AMD SEV-SNP（安全加密虚拟化）防护机制（来源：[3]）。SEV-SNP 是 AMD 在机密计算和云端企业级市场的核心卖点，此漏洞的曝光对其在数据中心市场的信任度构成了实质性打击。
2.  **地缘政治与主权云的连带风险**：2026年5月16日，业界广泛讨论了欧洲建设主权云以摆脱美国控制的战略，但媒体尖锐指出，这些主权云项目严重忽视了底层处理器（如 AMD 等 x86 架构）自带的安全风险。结合上述 Infinity Fabric 漏洞，AMD 在欧洲等对数据安全极度敏感的政府级采购中可能会面临更严格的审查。
3.  **底层编译器的持续演进**：在开发者生态方面，社区在 2026年5月8日 高度关注了 QBE（轻量级高性能编译器后端项目）。虽然这不是 AMD 官方的直接动作，但反映了非 NVIDIA 阵营的开发者正在积极寻找更轻量、更高效的底层编译替代方案，以期在未来打破 CUDA 的垄断。

### 总结

综合来看，NVIDIA 正在通过“硬件霸权 + 400亿美元级资本绑定 + Fleet Intelligence 等集群软件”构建多维度的市场壁垒，其市场表现极具进攻性。而 AMD 目前的市场表现偏向防守，虽然其在数据中心市场仍是唯一有体量的替代者，但近期 Infinity Fabric 的安全漏洞暴露了其在底层架构严谨性上的短板。AMD 亟需在修复安全信任危机的同时，加快其在 AI 软件栈和编译器后端的生态建设，否则两者的市场表现差距在短期内将进一步拉大。

evidence.title: QBE – Compiler Back End
evidence.title_cn: [开发] QBE：轻量级高性能编译器后端项目
evidence.summary: QBE是一款C语言编写的轻量级编译器后端，旨在以10%的代码量实现工业级编译器70%的性能。该项目保持极小体积，专注于核心优化，支持完整的C ABI及IEEE浮点数规范。其基于SSA的统一中间语言贯穿所有编译阶段，具备拷贝消除、稀疏条件常量传播、智能溢出启发及线性寄存器分配等优化特性。目前支持amd64、arm64和riscv64架构，并提供极快的编译速度，适合用于语言创新与快速集成。

evidence.title: Introducing NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence for Real-Time GPU Fleet Visibility and Optimization
evidence.title_cn: [硬件] NVIDIA推出Fleet Intelligence服务实现GPU集群实时监控与优化
evidence.summary: NVIDIA正式推出Fleet Intelligence托管服务，旨在为数据中心GPU/CPU集群提供实时监控与优化能力。该服务基于开源Agent，整合GPUd、DCGM及Attestation SDK技术，聚焦功耗、温度、性能、健康状态与安全验证，支持Blackwell、Hopper及Vera Rubin架构。通过可视化面板、实时告警与完整性校验，帮助企业提升算力资源利用率与运维可靠性。

evidence.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
evidence.title_cn: [商业] Nvidia年初已在AI领域投入超400亿美元股权投资
evidence.summary: 据CNBC报道，Nvidia在2026年初已向AI企业承诺超400亿美元股权投资。其中最大单笔投资为向OpenAI注资300亿美元，此外还包括向玻璃制造商Corning投资32亿美元及数据中心运营商IREN投资21亿美元。数据显示，Nvidia今年已参与约24轮私营初创公司融资。尽管外界批评此类投资属于资金在供需双方间循环的循环交易，但分析师指出此举有助于构建竞争优势护城河。

evidence.title: Making LLM Training Faster with Unsloth and NVIDIA
evidence.title_cn: [开发] Unsloth与NVIDIA合作优化LLM训练管线，综合提速约25%
evidence.summary: Unsloth与NVIDIA合作针对LLM训练管线实施三项底层优化，综合提升训练速度约25%。首项为打包序列元数据缓存，避免跨层重复构建，在Qwen3-14B QLoRA微调中实现前向传播提速43.3%、单批次提速14.3%。第二项采用双缓冲梯度检查点重载技术，实现数据拷贝与反向计算重叠，在8B至32B密集模型中取得4.6%至8.4%的吞吐量提升，显存额外开销仅0.2至0.5GB。第三项重构MoE路由逻辑，将动态查询开销降至常数级，路由路径前向与反向分别提速23%与13%。

evidence.title: Europe built sovereign clouds to escape US control. Forgot about the processors
evidence.title_cn: [安全] 欧洲建主权云欲摆脱美国控制，却忽视处理器底层安全风险
evidence.summary: 欧洲投入超20亿欧元推进主权云计划，并推出SecNumCloud框架以规避美国《云法案》及RISAA 2024等长臂管辖。然而，数据中心底层仍依赖Intel ME与AMD PSP处理器，其Ring -3管理引擎独立于操作系统运行，存在不受框架覆盖的固件级安全风险。专家指出该架构可被强制要求提供后门访问，且网络流量难以隔离。尽管现行运营控制可降低攻击面，但芯片层主权缺失仍是欧洲数字主权建设的根本隐患。

evidence.title: Fabricked: Misconfiguring Infinity Fabric to Break AMD SEV-SNP
evidence.title_cn: [安全] 研究人员披露利用Infinity Fabric错误配置攻破AMD SEV-SNP的漏洞
evidence.summary: 安全团队披露CVE-2025-54510漏洞“Fabricked”，该漏洞利用AMD Infinity Fabric总线错误配置破坏SEV-SNP机密计算环境。攻击者篡改UEFI与监控程序重定向PSP内存写入，致使RMP访问控制表初始化失效，实现CVM内存任意读写。该纯软件漏洞成功率为百分之百，无需物理接触，影响Zen 3至Zen 5架构EPYC处理器，AMD已发布固件修复。

question: AI 芯片赛道目前的竞争格局如何
answer:
当前AI芯片赛道的竞争格局呈现出“一超多强、云厂商自研加速、新锐独角兽崛起”的复杂态势。根据ASML的预测，整个行业的芯片供应受限状态还将持续2至5年，这进一步加剧了各方对算力资源的争夺。以下是目前AI芯片市场的核心竞争格局与具体动态：

一、 英伟达（NVIDIA）维持霸主地位，生态壁垒依然稳固
英伟达在AI训练和推理市场仍占据主导地位。尽管面临多方挑战，但其硬件性能与CUDA软件生态的护城河依然深厚。根据SemiAnalysis在2026年4月30日的实测数据，AMD的MI300X在训练性能上仍不及英伟达的H100，且软件生态依然是AMD的明显短板。
同时，英伟达正在积极推进下一代架构的落地。各大云厂商均承诺将在年内接入其最新的Vera Rubin芯片。此外，英伟达还在向政府与国防安全领域渗透，于2026年5月1日与美国国防部、微软及AWS签署协议，在机密网络中部署AI技术。

二、 云服务巨头（CSP）加速自研芯片，主攻性价比与推理市场
为了降低对英伟达的依赖并控制算力成本，谷歌、亚马逊等云巨头正在大规模部署自研AI芯片，并开始在内部及大客户中替代部分英伟达份额。
1. 谷歌（Google）：2026年4月22日，谷歌云正式发布了第八代定制AI芯片，并将其明确划分为面向模型训练的TPU 8t与针对推理的TPU 8i。新一代芯片的训练速度提升了3倍，每美元性能提高80%，单集群可支持超100万颗TPU协同工作。不过，谷歌也明确表示该系列旨在补充而非替代英伟达生态，并正与英伟达合作优化开源网络技术Falcon（详情见：[1] ）。
2. 亚马逊（AWS）：AWS的自研芯片正在赢得头部科技公司的青睐。2026年4月24日，Meta与AWS签署协议，采购数百万颗Graviton CPU以支持其AI推理及智能体工作负载。这一举措不仅将Meta的部分云预算从谷歌云转移至AWS，也直接对标了英伟达的Vera CPU，证明了AWS在价格性能比上的强劲竞争力。

三、 新锐独角兽强势破局，Cerebras成最大黑马
在初创与独立芯片供应商阵营中，Cerebras Systems凭借与OpenAI的深度绑定迅速崛起。2026年5月14日，Cerebras成功完成IPO，发行价定为185美元，融资总额高达55亿美元，完全稀释后估值达到564亿美元。
Cerebras的财务数据表现亮眼，其2025年营收达到5.1亿美元（同比增长76%），净利润为2.378亿美元，成功实现财务扭亏。该公司目前高度聚焦AI推理芯片业务，不仅获得了OpenAI超100亿美元的多年期合作协议及10亿美元贷款，其核心客户群还扩展至G42、MBZUAI以及AWS。

四、 边缘AI与代工制造格局的演进
在先进制程与代工端，台积电（TSMC）依然严防死守其技术机密（近期有前员工因泄露2nm制程机密被判处最高10年有期徒刑），而苹果则在2026年5月8日与英特尔（Intel）达成了初步的芯片制造协议，显示出头部企业在供应链多元化上的布局。
在边缘端与专用芯片领域，创新也在持续涌现。例如，安克创新（Anker）在4月22日发布了首款存算一体AI音频芯片Thus，表明AI芯片正在向更细分的端侧应用场景垂直渗透。

总结而言，当前的AI芯片赛道已经从单纯的“算力堆叠”演变为涵盖底层制程、软件生态、能耗比（每美元性能）以及资本绑定的全方位博弈。英伟达守卫高端训练市场，云厂商通过自研芯片蚕食推理与高性价比市场，而以Cerebras为代表的新势力则借助头部AI大厂的扶持重塑了行业版图。

AI芯片赛道涉及众多细分领域和参与者。请问您是希望重点了解传统芯片巨头（如英伟达）的市场地位，还是更关注云服务商（如谷歌、微软、AWS）自研芯片对当前竞争格局的影响？

evidence.title: Here’s how our TPUs power increasingly demanding AI workloads.
evidence.title_cn: [硬件] 谷歌详解TPU架构与算力演进
evidence.summary: 谷歌介绍其专为AI负载定制的TPU芯片架构。该处理器专为大规模矩阵运算设计，最新一代算力达121 exaflops，内存带宽较前代提升一倍。TPU已在谷歌基础设施内部署十余年，主要为训练与推理日益复杂的AI模型提供专用硬件算力支撑。

evidence.title: Accelerating Frontier Transformation with Microsoft partners
evidence.title_cn: [AI] 微软发布前沿转型框架与Agent 365套件，推动合作伙伴生态规模化落地
evidence.summary: 微软发布“前沿转型”框架及Microsoft 365 E7与Agent 365套件，计划于2026年5月全面上市。该套件集成Copilot与Agent 365控制平面，旨在为AI Agent提供统一治理与安全底座。数据显示，超90%财富500强企业已采用Copilot，IDC预测2028年AI Agent数量将达13亿。微软同步更新合作伙伴计划与专家认证，依托Marketplace构建近3000亿美元生态商机，推动中小企业与渠道伙伴实现AI规模化落地。

evidence.title: Exclusive: Google deepens Thinking Machines Lab ties with new multi-billion-dollar deal
evidence.title_cn: [商业] 谷歌与Thinking Machines Lab达成数十亿美元云服务协议
evidence.summary: Thinking Machines Lab与谷歌云签署单笔数十亿美元的AI基础设施协议，将接入基于英伟达GB300 GPU的AI系统及云端训练部署服务。该公司由前OpenAI高管创立，曾以120亿美元估值完成20亿美元种子轮融资，并于十月推出自动化前沿模型构建工具“Tinker”。此次合作将支撑其强化学习工作负载，凸显了大模型训练对算力的高额需求。

evidence.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
evidence.title_cn: [硬件] 谷歌云推出两款新AI芯片TPU 8t与8i，兼顾训练与推理
evidence.summary: 谷歌云发布第八代定制AI芯片，正式分为面向模型训练的TPU 8t与针对推理的TPU 8i。新芯片宣称训练速度提升3倍，每美元性能提高80%，单集群可支持超100万颗TPU协同工作。谷歌明确表示该系列旨在补充而非替代英伟达生态，并承诺年内上线英伟达最新Vera Rubin芯片。此外，双方正合作优化开源网络技术Falcon，以提升英伟达系统在谷歌云中的运行效率。

evidence.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
evidence.title_cn: [硬件] 分析师称OpenAI正联合联发科与高通研发AI智能手机
evidence.summary: 分析师郭明錤指出，OpenAI正与联发科、高通及立讯精密合作开发智能手机。该设备预计以AI智能体替代传统应用，采用端云混合模型持续理解用户上下文。相关规格与供应链计划于2026年底至2027年初敲定，2028年量产。此举旨在突破现有系统限制并获取用户数据，公司此前已确认2026年下半年将发布首款硬件产品。

evidence.title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
evidence.title_cn: [AI] OpenAI对网络安全工具GPT-5.5 Cyber实施定向访问限制
evidence.summary: OpenAI宣布推出面向专业网络安全领域的GPT-5.5 Cyber模型，该工具具备渗透测试、漏洞识别利用及恶意软件逆向工程能力。出于防范恶意滥用的考量，OpenAI目前采取定向开放策略，用户需在线提交资质证明与使用计划方可申请访问。公司正与美国政府及相关机构协作，以逐步扩大合规用户范围。此前OpenAI曾公开批评Anthropic对同类工具的限制策略，此次亦转向相同准入机制。

evidence.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
evidence.title_cn: [商业] 美国国防部与英伟达、微软及AWS签约，在机密网络部署AI技术
evidence.summary: 美国国防部近日与英伟达、微软、AWS及Reflection AI签署协议，将相关AI技术与模型部署于IL6与IL7级机密网络，旨在打造“AI优先”作战力量并防范供应商锁定。该举措源于五角大楼此前与Anthropic在模型使用条款及安全护栏上的法律纠纷。目前，国防部GenAI.mil平台已为超130万人员提供非机密任务的文档起草与数据分析服务。

evidence.title: Microsoft’s AI data center push is colliding with its clean power goals
evidence.title_cn: [商业] 微软AI数据中心扩张与清洁能源目标冲突，或推迟小时级清洁电力匹配计划
evidence.summary: 微软内部正讨论是否推迟或缩减2030年“100%小时级清洁电力匹配”目标。因AI数据中心快速扩张带来巨大能耗压力，该严格指标被视为发展阻碍。微软官方仅确认维持年度清洁电力匹配承诺。为应对供电缺口，微软正与雪佛龙等合作在德州建设5吉瓦天然气发电厂。数据中心能耗激增正促使科技巨头转向天然气，此举可能引发公众对污染与电价的担忧，进而削弱微软推广新建数据中心的公众说服力。

evidence.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
evidence.title_cn: [AI] OpenAI在API中推出新型语音智能功能
evidence.summary: OpenAI在API中新增三项语音智能功能。其中GPT-Realtime-2具备GPT-5级推理能力，旨在处理复杂交互；GPT-Realtime-Translate支持70种输入语言与13种输出语言的实时翻译；GPT-Realtime-Whisper提供实时语音转文本能力。上述模型均集成至Realtime API，翻译与语音转写按分钟计费，新语音模型按Token消耗计费。系统已内置内容安全护栏，主要面向客户服务、教育及媒体等行业。

evidence.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
evidence.title_cn: [AI] Anthropic产品负责人Cat Wu：未来AI将在用户意识到需求前主动响应
evidence.summary: Anthropic正推进约950亿美元融资，企业级市场份额自2025年5月起已实现四倍增长。产品负责人Cat Wu表示，公司战略聚焦保持技术前沿而非跟随竞品。未来半年产品重心将转向主动式AI，实现从同步交互向自动化例程与智能体管理的演进。Anthropic强调管理者需具备领域专业知识以调试智能体指令，公司旨在通过自动化工具消除重复性任务以提升整体生产力。

evidence.title: Claude for Small Business
evidence.title_cn: [AI] Anthropic推出面向中小企业的Claude智能体工作流套件
evidence.summary: Anthropic推出面向小微企业的AI应用方案，深度集成QuickBooks、PayPal、HubSpot等商业软件。该套件内置十五项开箱即用的智能体工作流，覆盖财务对账、营销投放与合同管理等核心业务环节。系统严格采用人工发起加前置审批机制，继承原有平台权限且默认不将企业数据用于模型训练。官方同步联合PayPal上线免费AI素养课程，并启动多城巡回线下工作坊以加速技术落地。

evidence.title: Agents for financial services and insurance
evidence.title_cn: [AI] Anthropic发布金融与保险行业AI智能体模板及微软办公插件
evidence.summary: Anthropic发布10款金融与保险行业AI智能体模板，涵盖投研、估值、客户背景审查及月结等场景，支持作为Claude Cowork插件或自主运行智能体。同步上线Microsoft 365系列办公插件，实现跨应用上下文自动流转。生态方面接入邓白氏、穆迪等八家数据供应商连接器及模型上下文协议应用，强化企业级数据访问。搭载Claude Opus 4.7模型在Vals AI金融智能体基准测试中获64.37%得分。

question: AI 数据中心面临的能源挑战有哪些
answer:
AI 数据中心在推动技术飞速发展的同时，正面临着前所未有的能源挑战。这些挑战不仅体现在绝对电力的巨大缺口上，还深刻影响着全球电网结构、科技巨头的气候承诺以及前沿硬件架构的演进。具体而言，AI 数据中心面临的能源挑战主要包括以下几个维度：

**一、 激增的绝对电力需求与区域电网过载**
AI 模型的训练和推理需要消耗海量电力，这使得区域电网面临极大的负荷压力。例如，内华达州的能源供应商 NV Energy 目前待处理的电力负荷请求已超过 22 吉瓦（GW），这一数字是硅谷度假胜地太浩湖（Lake Tahoe）峰值用电量的 40 倍。在邻近的犹他州，近期更是批准了一个耗电量高达 9 吉瓦的数据中心项目，而该州目前的总用电量仅为 4 吉瓦。这种极端的供需失衡不仅挤压了常规民用和商业电网资源，还直接推高了当地的电价[1]。

**二、 清洁能源目标的妥协与对化石燃料的持续依赖**
科技巨头曾制定了激进的碳中和目标，但 AI 数据中心的扩张正与这些目标发生严重冲突。以微软为例，该公司内部正在讨论是否推迟或缩减其 2030 年实现“100%小时级清洁电力匹配”的严格目标。为了填补巨大的电力缺口，微软正与雪佛龙（Chevron）及 Engine No. 1 合作，在德克萨斯州西部建设一座装机容量高达 5 吉瓦的天然气发电厂。


**三、 散热瓶颈与极端场景的探索**
除了供电，能源转化为算力后产生的巨大热量也带来了严峻的冷却挑战。为了突破地球表面的能源和物理限制，Google Cloud 首席运营官 Francis deSouza 证实，谷歌正在探索建设“太空轨道数据中心”（orbital data centers）的可能性，以期在太空中获取更充沛的太阳能。然而，太空的真空环境消除了空气对流，数据中心只能依靠辐射来散发热量，这比目前地面数据中心依赖的空气和液冷系统要缓慢且困难得多，构成了全新的工程挑战。

**四、 软硬件协同的能效极限**
面对能源短缺，企业必须在架构层面榨干每一瓦特电力的算力（watts per flop）。谷歌等企业正通过全栈自研来提升能效，例如通过定制的 TPU 芯片来运行 Gemini 模型，其能效表现远超采购现成商用芯片的配置。然而，随着摩尔定律放缓和 ASML 等设备商预测的未来 2 至 5 年芯片供应受限，单纯依靠硬件迭代来对冲能源消耗的路径正变得越来越艰难。

**五、 公众压力与资源消耗的公关危机**
数据中心的能源消耗引发了公众对电价上涨、环境污染以及水资源消耗的广泛担忧。尽管有数据显示 AI 的耗水危机在一定程度上被媒体夸大（例如美国 AI 数据中心目前日均消耗淡水约 1060 万加仑，仅占全国总消耗的 0.008%，预计到 2030 年也仅升至 0.08%），但如果科技巨头因为 AI 扩张而放弃清洁能源承诺并转向天然气等化石燃料，将极大地削弱其在新建数据中心时的公众说服力，进而面临更严格的监管和社区阻力。

综上所述，AI 数据中心的能源挑战已经超越了单纯的电力采购问题，演变成一场涉及电网基建、气候政策、半导体供应链以及前沿物理探索的系统性危机。
